forms-workflow-028.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Secondary Window ek aisa box hai Smart Form mein jiska size aur position pehle se fix hota hai — aur yeh kabhi bhi apni jagah se hilta nahi aur na hi barhta hai. Socho ek physical letter — company ka letterhead hamesha upar left corner mein hota hai — woh kabhi neeche nahi jaata, zyada space nahi leta. Yahi hai Secondary Window. Agar tum usme zyada content daalo jo us box mein fit nahi hota — toh content cut off ho jaata hai, overflow nahi hota. Primary use case: company logo top-right mein, company name aur address top-left mein, page number footer mein, document title area mein. Yeh window har page pe dikhi tab tak jab tak tum page-specific settings se rokho nahi. Main window se farq yeh hai ki Main Window scroll aur grow kar sakti hai with content — Secondary Window bilkul fixed rehti hai, ek inch bhi zyada nahi leti. Interview tip: agar koi pooche 'logo aur header kahan rakhte ho Smart Form mein?' — iska jawab hai Secondary Window.
forms-workflow-028.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Interview mein Secondary Window poochha jaaye toh sirf 'fixed window hai' bolna kafi nahi. Structured explanation do:

1. Definition:
   Secondary Window ek fixed-size, fixed-position window hai Smart Form ke page pe. Iska X position, Y position, width aur height form design time pe define hota hai aur runtime pe change nahi hota.

2. Content Behavior:
   - Secondary Window mein content overflow nahi hota. Agar text ya data window ki height se zyada ho jaaye — extra content simply cut off ho jaata hai, agle page pe nahi jaata.
   - Isliye Secondary Window mein sirf woh content daalo jo amount mein predictable ho — company name 2 lines, address 3 lines, logo image.

3. Page Appearance:
   - Secondary Window default mein har page pe appear hoti hai jahan woh assign ki gayi hai.
   - Page-specific control ke liye form ke page node mein specific pages assign karte hain.

4. Typical Use Cases:
   - Company logo (top-right corner)
   - Document title — 'SALES INVOICE', 'PURCHASE ORDER'
   - Company name, address, GSTIN in header area
   - Page number / total pages in footer — 'Page 1 of 3'
   - Document date, reference number in a fixed corner

5. Contrast with Main Window:
   - Main Window grows with content — 50 invoice lines ho ya 500, Main Window sab accommodate karti hai across multiple pages.
   - Secondary Window never grows — always exactly the size you defined.

6. Practical Design Tip:
   Ek page mein ek Main Window honi chahiye (mandatory for multi-page forms) aur baaki sab Secondary Windows ho sakti hain for fixed areas. Secondary Windows Main Window ke saath overlap nahi karni chahiye — overlap se layout issues aate hain.
forms-workflow-028.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'A Secondary Window in Smart Forms has a fixed size and position defined at design time. It does not scroll or expand with content — if the content exceeds the window boundaries, it gets cut off rather than flowing to the next page. Secondary Windows are used for static, predictable content like the company logo, document header with company name and address, or the page number in the footer. Unlike the Main Window which can span multiple pages, a Secondary Window appears at its fixed position on each page and never overflows.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein 'fixed size, does not overflow, used for logos and headers' — yeh teen points clearly bolna. Phir contrast dena: 'Main Window grows with data, Secondary Window stays fixed.' Ye clear comparison strong answer banata hai.
forms-workflow-028.codeExamples:
" === Secondary Window Usage in Smart Form (Design Notes) ===
" Transaction: SMARTFORMS → Open/Create Form → Page node

" Step 1: In page node, right-click → Create → Secondary Window
" Define window properties:
"   Name:     HEADER_WIN
"   Position: Left = 1.00 cm, Top = 0.50 cm
"   Width:    19.00 cm
"   Height:   3.00 cm

" Step 2: Add content nodes inside Secondary Window
" Typical nodes in HEADER_WIN:
"   - Text node: Company Name (font size 14, bold)
"   - Text node: Company Address line 1
"   - Text node: Company Address line 2
"   - Graphic node: Company Logo (BDS stored graphic)

" Step 3: Footer window (another Secondary Window)
"   Name:     FOOTER_WIN
"   Position: Left = 1.00 cm, Top = 27.50 cm
"   Width:    19.00 cm
"   Height:   1.00 cm

" Step 4: Inside FOOTER_WIN — text node with system fields
" Content: 'Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&'
" SFSY-PAGE = current page number (Smart Form system field)
" SFSY-FORMPAGES = total pages

" Step 5: ABAP driver — no special handling needed for Secondary Windows
" Secondary Windows are purely form-design concerns
" Driver passes data; form layout handles window rendering
CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name
  EXPORTING
    control_parameters = ls_ctrl
    is_company         = ls_company_data
  TABLES
    it_items           = lt_items
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS             = 1.
forms-workflow-028.interviewScriptHinglish: SmartForms mein Secondary Window ek fixed-position window hai jo har page pe same coordinates pe repeat hota hai aur overflow nahi hota. Yeh typically headers, footers, logos, company address, ya page numbers ke liye use hota hai. Iska size predefined hota hai; agar content exceed kare toh overflow silently clip ho jaata hai. Conditions tab ke through conditional rendering appearance restrict kar sakti hai (for example, first page only). Mere project mein company logo aur address ek Secondary Window mein the taaki har page ke top-right pe consistently appear hote rahein. Business value reliable static branding aur reference info hai bina overflow surprises ke.
forms-workflow-028.interviewScriptEnglish: A Secondary Window in SmartForms is a fixed-position window that repeats at the same coordinates on every page and does not overflow. It is typically used for headers, footers, logos, company address, or page numbers. Its size is predefined; if content exceeds it, the overflow is silently clipped. Conditional rendering through the Conditions tab can restrict appearance (for example, first page only). In my project the company logo and address sat in a Secondary Window so they appeared consistently at the top-right of every page. The business value is reliable static branding and reference info without overflow surprises.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Fixed-position window repeating per page; no overflow.
Per-node setting to render only when condition is true (e.g., first page).
Current page number; usable for first-page logic.
Total page count; useful for 'Page X of Y' displays.
Width and height set at design time; clipping if content exceeds.
Hardcoded label or instruction usually placed in Secondary Window.
Graphic node uploaded via SE78 and referenced in a window.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Putting long dynamic text in a Secondary Window.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Content can exceed window size and silently clip without warning.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Keep Secondary Windows for short static content; long dynamic text belongs in Main Window.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Placing items table in a Secondary Window.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Secondary Windows do not overflow; rows beyond first page are lost.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Put repeating tables in the Main Window.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Forgetting to set first-page condition for a cover-page-only Secondary Window.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Cover content like 'Customer Original' appears on every page.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use SFSY-PAGE = 1 condition on the node to limit appearance to first page.

Used for:

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens if content exceeds a Secondary Window's size in Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Secondary Window ki defined boundaries exceed karne wala content simply clip ho jaata hai — yeh window ke andar next line pe ya next page pe overflow nahi karta. Koi error ya exception raise nahi hoti. Yeh critical design consideration hai: Secondary Windows mein sirf woh content hona chahiye jiska size design time pe known aur bounded ho, jaise fixed-line company headers, logos, ya page numbers. Dynamic content jo grow kar sakta hai Main Window mein jaana chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Content that exceeds a Secondary Window's defined boundaries is simply clipped — it does not overflow to the next line within the window or to the next page. There is no error or exception raised. This is a critical design consideration: Secondary Windows should only contain content whose size is known and bounded at design time, such as fixed-line company headers, logos, or page numbers. Dynamic content that can grow should go in the Main Window.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek project mein developer ne delivery address ko Secondary Window mein daala. Short addresses sahi dikhe, lekin 5-line international addresses mein last 2 lines cut off ho jaati thi. Fix: address content ko Main Window ke upar wale fixed section mein move kiya ya Secondary Window ki height badhayi.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Dynamic length wala content — jaise customer address, item description — Secondary Window mein rakhna. Kuch customers ke liye sahi dikta hai, kuch ke liye cut off hota hai. Always use Main Window for variable-length content.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Design guideline — what NOT to put in Secondary Window:
" BAD: customer remarks (can be 1 line or 10 lines)
" BAD: dynamic item list
" BAD: long terms and conditions text

" GOOD content for Secondary Window:
" Company name (fixed 1-2 lines)
" Logo graphic (fixed pixel size)
" Page number: 'Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&'
" Document type label: 'ORIGINAL INVOICE'

" To test: in SMARTFORMS test, use longest possible address
" If content gets clipped → move to Main Window
" If content always fits → Secondary Window is safe

followupAnswerBank.question: Can a Secondary Window appear on only some pages of a Smart Form?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, Secondary Window ka page-level appearance form structure mein assigned page nodes ke through controlled hota hai. Separate page nodes create karke — for example FIRST_PAGE aur NEXT_PAGE — aur header Secondary Window ko sirf FIRST_PAGE pe assign karke, logo aur header sirf page one pe appear karte hain. NEXT_PAGE node ke paas different, simpler window set hoga. Yeh per-page layout pe full control deta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, a Secondary Window's page-level appearance is controlled by which page nodes it is assigned to in the form structure. By creating separate page nodes — for example FIRST_PAGE and NEXT_PAGE — and assigning the header Secondary Window only to FIRST_PAGE, the logo and header appear only on page one. The NEXT_PAGE node would have a different, simpler window set. This gives full control over per-page layout.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client requirement: company logo aur full header sirf page 1 pe, continuation pages pe sirf 'Page X of Y' footer ho. Solution: do page nodes — PAGE1 mein LOGO_WIN aur HEADER_WIN assign, PAGE_NEXT mein sirf FOOTER_WIN assign. PAGE1 ka 'next page' = PAGE_NEXT set kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf ek page node banana aur expect karna ki window automatically kuch pages pe chhup jaaye — yeh nahi hota. Page-specific window control ke liye multiple page nodes banana mandatory hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Page structure in SMARTFORMS (GUI configuration):

" Page node: PAGE1
"   Windows assigned:
"     HEADER_WIN  (logo + company name)
"     MAIN_WIN    (document content)
"     FOOTER_WIN  (page number)
"   Next page: PAGE_NEXT

" Page node: PAGE_NEXT
"   Windows assigned:
"     CONT_HEADER_WIN  (smaller header: 'Continued')
"     MAIN_WIN         (same main window — continues content)
"     FOOTER_WIN       (page number)
"   Next page: PAGE_NEXT  (loops to itself for all subsequent pages)

" Result:
" Page 1: Full logo + header visible
" Page 2+: Minimal header, just continuation marker

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between a Secondary Window and the Main Window in terms of page overflow?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main Window SmartForms mein only window type hai jo pages ke across content overflow support karta hai. Jab Main Window mein content page boundary exceed karta hai, SmartForms automatically new page create karta hai aur wahan content rendering continue karta hai. Secondary Windows mein overflow capability nahi hoti — woh apne assigned page pe fixed position pe ek baar render hote hain, aur boundary ke beyond koi bhi content clip ho jaata hai. Yahi reason hai invoice line items Main Window mein jaate hain jabki header logo Secondary Window mein.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The Main Window is the only window type in Smart Forms that supports content overflow across pages. When content in the Main Window exceeds the page boundary, Smart Forms automatically creates a new page and continues rendering content there. Secondary Windows have no overflow capability — they are rendered once at their fixed position on their assigned page, and any content beyond the boundary is clipped. This is why invoice line items go in the Main Window while the header logo goes in a Secondary Window.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: 50-item purchase order print karna tha. Developer ne galti se items table ko ek Secondary Window mein daala — sirf first 8 items print hue jo window mein fit hue, baki 42 items silently cut off. Fix: items table ko Main Window mein move kiya — sab pages pe correctly print hone laga.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Repeating table data ko Secondary Window mein rakhna — classic mistake jo partial printing cause karta hai bina any error message ke.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Smart Form window type comparison:

" MAIN WINDOW:
"   - Exactly ONE per page node
"   - Content overflows to next page automatically
"   - Use for: tables with variable rows, long text paragraphs
"   - In form tree: always named MAIN or custom name with type=Main

" SECONDARY WINDOW:
"   - Multiple allowed per page node
"   - Content is clipped at window boundary — NO overflow
"   - Use for: company logo, fixed header, page number footer
"   - In form tree: type=Secondary

" ABAP code in driver is same for both — driver just passes data
" Window behavior difference is purely in form design settings
" Rule: if content row count varies at runtime → Main Window
"        if content is always same length → Secondary Window is fine

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you place a company logo in a Secondary Window in Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Secondary Window mein company logo place karne ke liye, main pehle logo graphic ko SAP ke BDS mein transaction SE78 use karke upload karta hoon, ek name specify karke aur use TIFF ya BMP ke roop mein store karke. SMARTFORMS mein, Secondary Window ke andar, main ek Graphic node create karta hoon aur BDS object name reference karta hoon. Main graphic dimensions ko Secondary Window boundaries ke andar fit karne ke liye set karta hoon. Logo phir har page pe fixed position pe render hota hai jahan Secondary Window assigned hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To place a company logo in a Secondary Window, I first upload the logo graphic to SAP's BDS using transaction SE78, specifying a name and storing it as a TIFF or BMP. In SMARTFORMS, inside the Secondary Window, I create a Graphic node and reference the BDS object name. I set the graphic dimensions to fit within the Secondary Window boundaries. The logo then renders at that fixed position on every page where the Secondary Window is assigned.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client ne new company logo upload karne ko kaha. SE78 mein old logo ka naam same rakha, naya BMP upload kiya — existing Smart Forms automatically new logo se print hone lage bina form change ke. Yeh BDS ka advantage hai — form code change kiye bina logo update ho gaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Logo file ko directly ABAP report mein hardcode karne ki koshish karna — BDS use karo. Ya logo dimensions Secondary Window se bade set karna — logo clip ho jaayega.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Step 1: Upload logo via SE78
" SE78 → GRAPHICS → BITMAPS → Create
" Object name: ZCOMPANY_LOGO
" Upload TIFF/BMP file (max 1MB recommended)

" Step 2: In SMARTFORMS → Secondary Window → Add Graphic node
" Graphic node settings:
"   Object class:  GRAPHICS
"   Object name:   ZCOMPANY_LOGO
"   Object type:   BITMAPS
"   Width:         4.00 cm
"   Height:        2.50 cm
"   Resolution:    150 dpi

" Step 3: Position Secondary Window to accommodate logo
"   Window position: Top=0.5cm, Left=15cm (right side)
"   Window size: Width=4.5cm, Height=3cm

" No ABAP code changes needed in driver — graphic is static
" Driver passes normal parameters; form handles logo rendering

followupAnswerBank.question: Can you have multiple Secondary Windows on the same page in Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, multiple Secondary Windows SmartForms mein same page pe coexist kar sakte hain. Typical invoice layout mein one page pe chaar Secondary Windows ho sakte hain: top-left pe company header window, top-right pe logo window, invoice number aur date ke liye mid-right pe document information window, aur bottom pe page numbers ke liye footer window. Har independently positioned, independently sized, aur apne text ya graphic nodes ke saath independently populated hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, multiple Secondary Windows can coexist on the same page in Smart Forms. A typical invoice layout might have four Secondary Windows on one page: a company header window at top-left, a logo window at top-right, a document information window at mid-right for invoice number and date, and a footer window at the bottom for page numbers. Each is independently positioned, independently sized, and independently populated with its own text or graphic nodes.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Complex invoice form mein 5 Secondary Windows thi ek page pe: company header, logo, 'bill to' address box, 'ship to' address box, aur footer. Carefully each window ka X/Y position aur dimensions define kiya so they don't overlap. Print preview mein sab perfectly aligned aaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Windows ka overlap hona — ek window doosri ke upar aa jaati hai, content mixed dikha karta hai. SMARTFORMS mein form test mein print preview se overlap check karo har change ke baad.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Multiple Secondary Windows on same page — position planning:

" Page size: A4 (21 cm wide x 29.7 cm tall)
" Margins: Left=1cm, Right=1cm, Top=1cm, Bottom=1cm
" Usable area: 19cm x 27.7cm

" Window layout (no overlaps):
" LOGO_WIN:    Left=15cm, Top=0.5cm, Width=4cm,  Height=2.5cm
" HEADER_WIN:  Left=1cm,  Top=0.5cm, Width=13cm, Height=2.5cm
" BILL_TO_WIN: Left=1cm,  Top=4cm,   Width=9cm,  Height=3cm
" SHIP_TO_WIN: Left=10cm, Top=4cm,   Width=9cm,  Height=3cm
" MAIN_WIN:    Left=1cm,  Top=8cm,   Width=19cm, Height=18cm
" FOOTER_WIN:  Left=1cm,  Top=27cm,  Width=19cm, Height=0.7cm

" Rule: sum of vertical positions + heights must not exceed page usable area
" Verify: no two windows have overlapping X+Width or Y+Height ranges

followupAnswerBank.question: What system variables can you use inside a Secondary Window for page numbers?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SmartForms SFSY structure provide karta hai runtime system fields contain karte hue jo form mein kahin bhi accessible hain. Footer Secondary Window mein page numbers ke liye, main current page ke liye SFSY-PAGE aur total page count ke liye SFSY-FORMPAGES use karta hoon. Text node mein, syntax 'Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&' hai jahan ampersands variable substitution denote karte hain. SFSY-DATE aur SFSY-TIME print date aur time dete hain. Yeh fields SmartForms runtime dwara automatically populated hote hain bina kisi driver code ke.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Smart Forms provides the SFSY structure containing runtime system fields accessible anywhere in the form. For page numbers in a footer Secondary Window, I use SFSY-PAGE for the current page and SFSY-FORMPAGES for the total page count. In a Text node, the syntax is 'Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&' where ampersands denote variable substitution. SFSY-DATE and SFSY-TIME give the print date and time. These fields are automatically populated by the Smart Forms runtime with no driver code needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Client requirement: footer mein 'Page 2 of 5' format chahiye tha. FOOTER_WIN mein text node banaya — 'Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&'. Multi-page test kiya — automatically correct page numbers aa rahe the.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: SFSY-FORMPAGES SY-PAGNO se confuse karna. SY-PAGNO standard ABAP page variable hai, Smart Forms mein iske jagah SFSY-PAGE use karo. SFSY-FORMPAGES total pages batata hai jo SY-PAGNO mein nahi hota.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Inside Secondary Window (FOOTER_WIN) — Text node content:
" 'Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&'
" Output example: 'Page 2 of 5'

" Other SFSY fields available in any window:
" &SFSY-DATE&         Current date (formatted per form settings)
" &SFSY-TIME&         Current time
" &SFSY-WORKDATE&     Work date
" &SFSY-USERNAME&     Logged-in user name
" &SFSY-SUBRC&        Last subrc

" Text node example combining multiple SFSY fields:
" 'Printed on &SFSY-DATE& by &SFSY-USERNAME& | Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&'

" No ABAP driver code needed — SFSY is auto-populated by Smart Forms runtime
" Just use &FIELDNAME& notation in text nodes

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you assign a Secondary Window to appear only on the first page?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Secondary Window ko sirf first page pe appear karne ke liye restrict karne ke liye, main form mein do page nodes create karta hoon: FIRST_PAGE aur NEXT_PAGE. Full company header wala elaborate Secondary Window sirf FIRST_PAGE pe assign hota hai. NEXT_PAGE pe different, simplified Secondary Window set hota hai ya bilkul nahi. FIRST_PAGE ka Next Page attribute NEXT_PAGE ko point karta hai, aur NEXT_PAGE ka Next Page khud ko point karta hai saare subsequent pages ke liye loop karne ke liye. Main Window dono pages pe assigned hota hai content continuity enable karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To restrict a Secondary Window to only the first page, I create two page nodes in the form: FIRST_PAGE and NEXT_PAGE. The elaborate Secondary Window with the full company header is assigned only to FIRST_PAGE. NEXT_PAGE has a different, simplified Secondary Window set or none at all. The FIRST_PAGE Next Page attribute points to NEXT_PAGE, and NEXT_PAGE's Next Page points to itself to loop for all subsequent pages. The Main Window is assigned to both pages to enable content continuity.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement: first page pe company ka full letterhead, subsequent pages pe sirf 'CONTINUATION - Invoice 12345' text in a minimal header. Two page nodes banaye, alag Secondary Windows assign kiye. Works perfectly for any number of pages.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Ek hi page node use karke conditional logic se window chhupane ki koshish karna — Smart Forms mein window-level conditions nahi hoti uss tarah se. Correct approach hai multiple page nodes banana.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Smart Form page node structure:

" Page node: FIRST_PAGE
"   Assigned Secondary Windows:
"     FULL_HEADER_WIN  (logo + full company header, 5cm height)
"     FOOTER_WIN       (page number)
"   Assigned Main Window:
"     MAIN             (invoice content starts here)
"   Next Page: NEXT_PAGE

" Page node: NEXT_PAGE
"   Assigned Secondary Windows:
"     MINI_HEADER_WIN  (just 'Continued - Invoice &IS_HEADER-VBELN&')
"     FOOTER_WIN       (page number — same window, reused)
"   Assigned Main Window:
"     MAIN             (content continues from previous page)
"   Next Page: NEXT_PAGE   ← loops to itself

" Key: Main Window must be on both pages for pagination to work
" Secondary Windows can differ between page nodes

followupAnswerBank.question: Why should you not put long dynamic text in a Secondary Window?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Secondary Windows ki fixed height hoti hai jo runtime pe expand nahi ho sakti. Agar dynamic text us fixed height ko exceed karta hai, overflow silently discard hota hai — koi exception nahi, koi error message nahi, aur output mein koi indication nahi ki content missing hai. Yeh particularly business-critical content jaise customer-specific delivery instructions, item remarks, ya legal terms ke liye dangerous hai jo length mein vary kar sakte hain. Aisa content Main Window mein jaana chahiye jahan overflow safely next page pe continue hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Secondary Windows have a fixed height that cannot expand at runtime. If dynamic text exceeds that fixed height, the overflow is silently discarded — there is no exception, no error message, and no indication in the output that content is missing. This is particularly dangerous for business-critical content like customer-specific delivery instructions, item remarks, or legal terms that can vary in length. Such content must go in the Main Window where overflow safely continues to the next page.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Purchase order mein vendor terms ko Secondary Window mein rakha tha. Short terms wale POs fine the. Ek vendor ke 2-page terms the — aadhe se zyada content missing tha printed PO mein. Legal risk tha. Fix: terms text ko Main Window ke end section mein move kiya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assume karna ki 'generally yeh content fit ho jaata hai toh chalega' — edge cases mein fail hoga. Always aur always dynamic-length content ko Main Window mein rakho.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Content placement decision matrix:

" SECONDARY WINDOW — safe for:
"   Company name         → always 1-2 lines, fixed
"   Company logo         → fixed pixel size
"   Document title       → 'INVOICE' or 'PURCHASE ORDER', 1 line
"   Page X of Y footer   → always 1 line
"   Fixed reference labels → 'Invoice No:', 'Date:' labels

" MAIN WINDOW — required for:
"   Line items table     → 1 to 1000+ rows, unpredictable
"   Customer remarks     → 0 to N lines
"   Terms & conditions   → variable paragraphs
"   Item long text       → can be empty or multi-paragraph
"   Payment terms block  → varies by customer agreement

" Test method: in driver, pass maximum possible content
" If form output shows truncation → move to Main Window
" Never trust 'it usually fits' for production forms

forms-workflow-029.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Copy Window ek aisi special window hai Smart Forms mein jo SIRF tab print hoti hai jab tum ek document ke multiple copies print kar rahe ho. Single copy print karo — Copy Window invisible rehti hai. Multiple copies print karo — Copy Window har duplicate copy pe dikhi deti hai, typically 'COPY' ya 'DUPLICATE' stamp ke saath. Real life mein socho: CA ne invoice ki 3 copies print ki — original client ke liye, ek account ke liye, ek file ke liye. Original pe kuch nahi, baaki dono pe 'COPY' stamped. Yahi Smart Form mein Copy Window karta hai — automatically original ko clean rakhta hai aur duplicates ko clearly mark karta hai. Common use: commercial invoice (original + 2 copies), challan (original + triplicate), government forms jahan multiple copies mandatory hain. Koi alag logic ya condition code likhne ki zaroorat nahi — SAP runtime automatically detect karta hai ki yeh first copy hai ya duplicate, aur accordingly Copy Window render karta hai ya skip karta hai.
forms-workflow-029.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Interview mein Copy Window poochha jaaye toh ek clear aur practical explanation do:

1. Definition:
   Copy Window ek Smart Form window type hai jo sirf non-first copies (i.e., duplicate copies) print hone pe visible hoti hai. Pehli copy (original) pe yeh window render hi nahi hoti.

2. How It Works Internally:
   SAP ke output options mein number of copies specify hoti hain. Smart Forms runtime ke paas yeh information hoti hai ki current printing pass kaunsi copy number hai. Agar copy number > 1 hai — Copy Window show hoti hai. Copy number = 1 (original) — Copy Window skip hoti hai.

3. Practical Content:
   Copy Window mein typically likha hota hai:
   - 'COPY' in large bold red text
   - 'DUPLICATE'
   - 'NOT FOR PAYMENT' (invoices ke liye)
   - Copy number: 'Copy 2 of 3'

4. Position:
   Copy Window usually page ke center ya top pe diagonally placed hoti hai — watermark style. Ya header section mein clearly visible.

5. Business Context:
   - Invoice: Original accounts receivable ke liye, COPY customer ke liye
   - Delivery note: Original transporter ke liye, COPY warehouse ke liye
   - Government customs documents: Specific copy numbers mandatory

6. Driver Program Side:
   Output options structure (SSFCOMPOP) mein tdcopies field se number of copies specify karte hain. Copy Window behavior automatic hai — developer ko koi extra code nahi likhna.

7. Key Differentiator from Final Window:
   Copy Window = multiple copies context (horizontal — same document, multiple printouts)
   Final Window = multi-page context (vertical — same printout, last page only)
forms-workflow-029.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'A Copy Window in Smart Forms is a special window that is rendered only when printing duplicate copies of a document, not on the first original copy. When a driver program requests multiple copies via the output options, SAP Smart Forms runtime automatically suppresses the Copy Window for copy number one and displays it for all subsequent copies. This is used to stamp duplicate copies with text like COPY or DUPLICATE without any conditional programming logic in the driver. Common use cases include commercial invoices, delivery challans, and regulatory documents requiring multiple differentiated copies.'

Hinglish tip: 'Copy Window automatically shows on duplicate copies, hidden on original — no IF conditions needed in code' — yeh point interviewer ko impress karta hai kyunki yeh declarative form design ki power dikhata hai.
forms-workflow-029.codeExamples:
" === Copy Window in Smart Forms — Design and Driver ===

" SMARTFORMS Form Design:
" Step 1: In page node, create a Copy Window
"   Window type: Copy Window (select from dropdown)
"   Name:        COPY_WIN
"   Position:    Left=5cm, Top=10cm  (center of page)
"   Width:       9cm, Height:3cm

" Step 2: Inside COPY_WIN, add a Text node
"   Content: 'COPY'
"   Font: Arial 48pt, Bold, Color=Red (or gray for subtle)
"   Or diagonal watermark style text

" Step 3: Driver program — set number of copies in output options
DATA: lv_fm_name TYPE rs38l_fnam,
      ls_ctrl    TYPE ssfctrlop,
      ls_out     TYPE ssfcompop.

CALL FUNCTION 'SSF_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME'
  EXPORTING  formname = 'ZINVOICE_FORM'
  IMPORTING  fm_name  = lv_fm_name
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS   = 1.

" Set 3 copies: 1 original + 2 copies
" Copy Window will appear on copy 2 and copy 3 automatically
ls_out-tdcopies  = '3'.        " Total copies to print
ls_out-tddest    = 'LP01'.     " Printer destination
ls_ctrl-no_dialog = abap_true. " Background — no dialog
ls_ctrl-preview   = abap_false.

CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name
  EXPORTING
    control_parameters = ls_ctrl
    output_options     = ls_out
    is_header          = ls_vbak
  TABLES
    it_items           = lt_vbap
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS             = 1.
" Result: 3 copies printed
" Copy 1 (original): COPY_WIN not shown
" Copy 2 & 3: COPY_WIN shows 'COPY' text

followupAnswerBank.question: How does Smart Forms know which copy is the original and which is a duplicate?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Smart Forms runtime internally har print pass ke dauran copy number track karta hai. Jab driver output options mein tdcopies 3 set karta hai, SAP same document ke liye teen rendering passes execute karta hai. Pass 1 ke dauran — original — Copy Windows suppress hote hain. Passes 2 aur 3 ke dauran, Copy Windows render hote hain. Yeh entirely Smart Forms engine ke through handle hota hai; developer is behavior ke liye koi conditional logic nahi likhta.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Smart Forms runtime internally tracks the copy number during each print pass. When the driver sets tdcopies to 3 in the output options, SAP executes three rendering passes for the same document. During pass 1 — the original — Copy Windows are suppressed. During passes 2 and 3, Copy Windows are rendered. This is handled entirely by the Smart Forms engine; the developer does not write any conditional logic for this behavior.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Testing ke liye driver mein tdcopies = 1 set kiya — print kiya. COPY stamp nahi dikha (correct — original). Phir tdcopies = 3 kiya — print kiya. First print clean, doosra aur teesra 'COPY' stamped. Confirmation ki Copy Window sahi kaam kar rahi hai.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: ABAP mein IF condition likhna Copy Window show/hide karne ke liye — yeh zaroorat nahi. Smart Forms automatically handle karta hai. Unnecessary code complexity create hoti hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Internal logic (conceptual — not actual SAP code you write):
" SAP Smart Forms runtime internally does:
" LOOP copy_number FROM 1 TO ls_out-tdcopies.
"   IF copy_number = 1.
"     render_copy_windows = abap_false.  " Original
"   ELSE.
"     render_copy_windows = abap_true.   " Duplicate copies
"   ENDIF.
"   render_form_page().
" ENDLOOP.

" What developer writes in driver — just set copies count:
ls_out-tdcopies = '3'.  " That's it! SAP handles the rest.

" No IF, no copy counter, no conditional window logic needed
" Copy Window in form design does everything automatically

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between Copy Window and a text node with a condition in Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Copy Window declarative, purpose-built window type hai jahan Smart Forms runtime copy number ke basis pe visibility automatically control karta hai — zero coding required. Manual condition wala text node developer ko explicit IS_COPY flag driver se pass karne aur condition logic likhne ki require karta hai, jo complex hai kyunki tumhe pata hona chahiye ki tum kaunse pass mein ho. Copy Window recommended approach hai — simpler, cleaner, aur koi driver code changes nahi chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: A Copy Window is a declarative, purpose-built window type where the Smart Forms runtime automatically controls visibility based on the copy number — zero coding required. A text node with a manual condition requires the developer to pass an explicit IS_COPY flag from the driver and write the condition logic, which is complex since you need to know which pass you are in. Copy Window is the recommended approach — simpler, cleaner, and no driver code changes needed.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Junior developer ne manual IS_COPY flag approach try ki — driver mein complex loop likha. Senior developer ne suggest kiya Copy Window use karo — 5 minute ka form change, driver code sab delete. Same result, much simpler implementation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Manual condition approach use karna jab Copy Window available hai — reinventing the wheel. Always use built-in window types for their intended purpose.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" WRONG approach — manual condition on text node:
" Driver sets is_copy = abap_true for copy 2, 3
" But how does driver know it is printing copy 2??
" Very complex, error-prone.

" CORRECT approach — use Copy Window:
" In SMARTFORMS → Page → Create → Copy Window
" Add 'COPY' text inside
" In driver — just set:
ls_out-tdcopies = '3'.
" Done. No conditions, no IS_COPY flag, no complex logic.

" Copy Window handles it all — original gets no stamp
" copies 2 and 3 get 'COPY' stamp automatically

followupAnswerBank.question: Can Copy Window have different content for each copy number?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Standard Smart Forms Copy Window saare duplicate copies pe same content dikhata hai — typically ek COPY ya DUPLICATE stamp. 'Copy 2 of 3' jaise numbered copy differentiation ke liye, tum SFSY system fields use kar sakte ho agar copy count accessible hai, ya Copy Window ke andar custom ABAP code nodes implement kar sakte ho jo copy-specific data reference karte hain. However, zyada real-world scenarios mein, saari non-original copies pe simple COPY stamp business requirement hai, aur standard Copy Window ise perfectly handle karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Standard Smart Forms Copy Window shows the same content on all duplicate copies — typically a COPY or DUPLICATE stamp. For numbered copy differentiation like 'Copy 2 of 3', you can use the SFSY system fields if the copy count is accessible, or implement custom ABAP code nodes inside the Copy Window that reference copy-specific data. However, in most real-world scenarios, a simple COPY stamp on all non-original copies is the business requirement, and the standard Copy Window handles this perfectly.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Ek government form requirement thi jisme 'ORIGINAL', 'OFFICE COPY', 'FILE COPY' alag-alag labels chahiye the. In case mein Copy Window standard approach se handle nahi hota tha — custom solution: 3 alag driver calls kiye with specific flags for each copy type.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assume karna ki Copy Window automatically 'Copy 1', 'Copy 2' numbered labels show karta hai — nahi karta. Sirf original vs duplicate distinguish karta hai. Numbered labels ke liye custom logic chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Standard Copy Window — same content all copies:
" Content: 'COPY' (appears on all duplicate copies)

" For numbered copies — custom approach in driver:
" Print 3 separate times with different parameters:
DATA lv_copy_label TYPE string.

DO 3 TIMES.
  CASE sy-index.
    WHEN 1. lv_copy_label = 'ORIGINAL'.
    WHEN 2. lv_copy_label = 'OFFICE COPY'.
    WHEN 3. lv_copy_label = 'FILE COPY'.
  ENDCASE.

  ls_out-tdcopies = '1'.  " 1 copy per call
  CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name
    EXPORTING
      iv_copy_label = lv_copy_label  " Pass to form
    EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 1.
ENDDO.
" Inside form: regular text node shows iv_copy_label value

followupAnswerBank.question: In what business scenarios is Copy Window most commonly used?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Copy Window most commonly multiple differentiated copies require karne wale commercial aur regulatory documents mein use hota hai. Key scenarios include: commercial invoices jahan original accounts receivable ko jaata hai aur copies customer aur internal file ko; delivery challans jahan transporter, warehouse, aur customer har ko ek copy chahiye; bank aur legal documents jahan clearly marked originals aur copies chahiye; aur government-mandated forms jaise customs declarations ya excise documents jahan clear ORIGINAL aur COPY markings wali specific number of copies legally required hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Copy Window is most commonly used in commercial and regulatory documents requiring multiple differentiated copies. Key scenarios include: commercial invoices where the original goes to accounts receivable and copies go to the customer and internal file; delivery challans where transporter, warehouse, and customer each need a copy; bank and legal documents needing clearly marked originals and copies; and government-mandated forms like customs declarations or excise documents where a specific number of copies with clear ORIGINAL and COPY markings is legally required.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Logistics project mein delivery note ke 3 copies print hote the — Copy Window ne original ko clean rakha, baaki 2 copies pe COPY stamp automatic aa gaya. Transporters aur warehouse staff ke liye clear distinction tha bina koi extra configuration ke.

Header approach COPY text ko document title ke pass top of the page pe dedicated box mein place karta hai. Dono approaches valid hain; choice client ke document standards aur visual preferences pe depend karti hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Copy Window is typically positioned either as a diagonal watermark across the page center or as a clearly visible stamp in the document header area. The diagonal watermark style places the Copy Window centrally with rotated text — visually obvious but content still readable. The header approach places COPY text in a dedicated box at the top of the page near the document title. Both approaches are valid; the choice depends on the client's document standards and visual preferences.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does Copy Window differ from Final Window in Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Copy Window aur Final Window Smart Forms mein different purposes serve karte hain. Copy Window print copies ke baare mein hai — yeh saare copies pe render hota hai siwaye first original ke jab multiple copies print hote hain. Final Window page position ke baare mein hai — yeh sirf multi-page document output ke last page pe render hota hai. Copy Window document copies ke horizontal dimension ko address karta hai; Final Window page sequence ke vertical dimension ko address karta hai. Dono same form mein coexist kar sakte hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Copy Window and Final Window serve different purposes in Smart Forms. Copy Window is about print copies — it renders on all copies except the first original when multiple copies are printed. Final Window is about page position — it renders only on the last page of a multi-page document output. Copy Window addresses the horizontal dimension of document copies; Final Window addresses the vertical dimension of page sequence. Both can coexist in the same form.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Invoice form mein dono windows thi. Copy Window: 'COPY' stamp on duplicate copies. Final Window: signature block on last page. Ek 5-page invoice ke 2 copies print kiya — original ki page 5 pe signature block, copies ki page 5 pe signature block + 'COPY' stamp. Both working correctly simultaneously.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Copy Window aur Final Window ke purpose confuse karna. Final Window ko 'last copy' ke liye use karne ki galat soch. Final Window = last PAGE. Copy Window = non-original COPY.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" In same Smart Form — both windows present:
" Page node: FIRST_PAGE
"   Secondary Windows: HEADER_WIN, FOOTER_WIN
"   Copy Window: COPY_WIN (shows 'COPY' on duplicate copies)
"   Final Window: FINAL_WIN (shows signature on last page)
"   Main Window: MAIN

" Rendering behavior matrix:
" | Scenario           | COPY_WIN | FINAL_WIN |
" |--------------------|----------|-----------|
" | Original, page 1   | Hidden   | Hidden    |
" | Original, page 3   | Hidden   | Hidden    |
" | Original, last pg  | Hidden   | SHOWN     |
" | Copy 2, page 1     | SHOWN    | Hidden    |
" | Copy 2, last pg    | SHOWN    | SHOWN     |

" Both windows can appear together on last page of a copy

followupAnswerBank.question: Does the Copy Window affect PDF generation from Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Smart Forms se OTF output use karte waqt Copy Window behavior PDF generation pe bhi apply hota hai. Agar tdcopies 3 set hai aur getotf true hai, runtime teen passes ke liye OTF data generate karta hai, passes 2 aur 3 pe Copy Window render hota hai. Agar tumhe single PDF file chahiye jisme saare copies hon, tumhe CONVERT_OTF se pehle saare passes ka OTF output collect karke merge karna padta hai. Yeh email attachment scenarios mein common complexity hai jahan saare copies ek PDF file mein hone chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Copy Window behavior applies to PDF generation as well when using OTF output from Smart Forms. If tdcopies is set to 3 and getotf is true, the runtime generates OTF data for three passes, with the Copy Window rendered on passes 2 and 3. If you need a single PDF file containing all copies, you must collect OTF output from all passes and merge them before CONVERT_OTF. This is a common complexity in email attachment scenarios where all copies must be in one PDF file.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Requirement: 3-copy invoice ek PDF mein email karo. Simple tdcopies=3 + getotf approach se 3 alag OTF data sets mila. Merge logic likhi — saari OTF tables append ki, ek CONVERT_OTF call kiya — single PDF with all 3 copies generated.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assume karna ki tdcopies=3 aur getotf=true automatically ek merged PDF dega. Yeh multiple separate OTF data sets deta hai. Merge karna manually karna padta hai for single PDF requirement.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Copy Window + PDF generation:
DATA: lt_otf_all  TYPE TABLE OF itcoo,
      lt_pdf      TYPE TABLE OF tline.

ls_ctrl-getotf   = abap_true.
ls_out-tdcopies  = '3'.  " 3 copies

" Single call — runtime handles copy passes internally
CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name
  EXPORTING
    control_parameters = ls_ctrl
    output_options     = ls_out
  IMPORTING
    job_output_info    = ls_job_info
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 1.

" ls_job_info-otfdata now contains OTF for ALL 3 copies combined
" Copy 1 pages + Copy 2 pages (with COPY stamp) + Copy 3 pages

" Convert combined OTF to single PDF:
CALL FUNCTION 'CONVERT_OTF'
  EXPORTING  format = 'PDF'
  TABLES
    otf   = ls_job_info-otfdata
    lines = lt_pdf
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 1.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test that a Copy Window is working correctly?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Copy Window test karne ke liye, main driver program ke output options mein tdcopies 2 ya 3 set karta hoon aur preview enable karke run karta hoon. First output document ko bina kisi Copy Window content ke dikhana chahiye, jabki second aur third COPY stamp display karne chahiye. Print preview mode mein, Smart Forms kabhi saare copies sequentially dikhata hai — main scroll karke verify karta hoon copy 1 pe koi stamp nahi hai aur subsequent copies pe hai. Main single copy (tdcopies=1) ke saath bhi test karta hoon yeh confirm karne ke liye ki Copy Window completely absent hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To test Copy Window, I set tdcopies to 2 or 3 in the output options of the driver program and run with preview enabled. The first output should show the document without any Copy Window content, while the second and third should display the COPY stamp. In print preview mode, Smart Forms sometimes shows all copies sequentially — I scroll through to verify copy 1 has no stamp and subsequent copies do. I also test with single copy (tdcopies=1) to confirm Copy Window is completely absent.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: QA testing mein Copy Window content wrong position pe aa raha tha — COPY text main content ke upar overlap ho raha tha. Print preview mein clearly dikha. Copy Window ki Y position adjust ki — overlap resolve hua.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf single copy (tdcopies=1) ke saath test karna — Copy Window test hi nahi hogi. Always test with minimum 2 copies to validate Copy Window behavior.

forms-workflow-030.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Final Window ek aisi Smart Form window hai jo SIRF document ke LAST page pe appear hoti hai. Intermediate pages pe yeh window completely hidden rehti hai. Socho ek legal contract — contract ki har page pe terms hoti hain (Main Window mein), lekin signature block sirf last page pe hoti hai. Yahi Final Window ka kaam hai — signature block, bank account details, important disclaimers, 'End of Document' markers — yeh sab sirf last page pe print ho. Iske bina kya hoga? Agar signature block har page pe print ho — multiple pages wale documents mein har page pe sign karna padega, jo obviously wrong hai. Final Window SAP ko automatically bolta hai: 'jab last page aaye tab mujhe render karo, baki pages pe mat dikhao.' Developer ko manually count karna nahi padta ki kaunsa page last hai — SAP runtime automatically detect karta hai.
forms-workflow-030.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Interview mein Final Window poochha jaaye toh detailed explanation do:

1. Definition:
   Final Window ek Smart Form window type hai jo sirf form output ke last page pe render hoti hai. Intermediate pages pe automatically skip hoti hai.

2. Why Final Window Exists:
   Multi-page documents mein kuch content logically sirf document ke end pe hona chahiye. Agar yeh content Main Window mein daalen — toh woh data flow ke saath jo bhi page pe aayega wahan print hoga, not necessarily last page. Final Window guarantee karta hai last page pe placement.

3. Common Final Window Content:
   - Signature block: 'Authorized Signatory ___________'
   - Terms and conditions (short version)
   - Bank account details for payment
   - 'End of Document' text
   - QR code ya barcode for document verification
   - Total amount in words (for invoices)
   - Seal/stamp area

4. Single Page Document Behavior:
   Agar form ka output sirf ek page hai — woh page same time first page bhi hai aur last page bhi. Final Window us single page pe appear hogi.

5. Technical Implementation:
   Smart Forms runtime page generation ke waqt yeh detect karta hai ki Main Window ka content khatam ho gaya — yeh last page hai. Us page pe Final Window render hoti hai.

6. Contrast with Secondary Window:
   Secondary Window = har assigned page pe dikhti hai.
   Final Window = sirf last page pe dikhti hai.

7. Practical Design Consideration:
   Final Window ke liye page pe enough space hona chahiye. Agar Main Window content itna zyada hai ki Final Window ke liye space hi nahi — Smart Forms automatically ek extra blank page create kar sakta hai sirf Final Window ke liye.
forms-workflow-030.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'A Final Window in Smart Forms is a special window that renders only on the last page of the form output. It does not appear on intermediate pages of a multi-page document. Typical content includes signature blocks, bank payment details, terms and conditions summary, or end-of-document markers. If the document is a single page, that single page is treated as both the first and last page, so the Final Window renders there. Smart Forms runtime automatically detects when Main Window content is exhausted and marks that as the last page, triggering Final Window rendering with no manual page counting needed in the driver program.'

Hinglish tip: 'Signature block sirf last page pe chahiye — Final Window use karo, koi manual page count nahi' — ye practical example aur technical simplicity dono ek saath explain karta hai.
forms-workflow-030.codeExamples:
" === Final Window in Smart Forms — Design and Usage ===

" SMARTFORMS Form Design:
" Step 1: In page node, right-click → Create → Final Window
"   Window type: Final Window (select from window type dropdown)
"   Name:        FINAL_WIN
"   Position:    Left=1cm, Top=24cm  (near bottom of page)
"   Width:       19cm, Height:4cm

" Step 2: Add content nodes inside FINAL_WIN:
"   Text node 1: 'For [Company Name]'
"   Text node 2: ''
"   Text node 3: '___________________________'
"   Text node 4: 'Authorized Signatory'
"   Text node 5: 'Date: ___________________'

" Optional: Bank details for invoice payment
"   Text node: 'Bank: HDFC Bank | A/C: 12345678 | IFSC: HDFC0001234'

" Step 3: Driver program — no special code for Final Window
" Smart Forms runtime automatically handles Final Window
DATA: lv_fm_name TYPE rs38l_fnam,
      ls_ctrl    TYPE ssfctrlop,
      ls_out     TYPE ssfcompop.

CALL FUNCTION 'SSF_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME'
  EXPORTING  formname = 'ZINVOICE_FORM'
  IMPORTING  fm_name  = lv_fm_name
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS   = 1.

ls_ctrl-preview   = abap_true.
ls_ctrl-no_dialog = abap_false.

" Pass all invoice data — Final Window rendering is automatic
CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name
  EXPORTING
    control_parameters = ls_ctrl
    output_options     = ls_out
    is_header          = ls_vbak
  TABLES
    it_items           = lt_vbap
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS             = 1.
" Result:
" Pages 1 to N-1: Main content only, no FINAL_WIN
" Last page N: Main content (remaining) + FINAL_WIN (signature block)
forms-workflow-030.interviewScriptHinglish: SmartForms mein Final Window form output ke sirf last page pe content render karta hai. Yeh typically grand totals, signature line, terms and conditions footer, ya stamp areas ke liye use hota hai. Single-page form mein Final Window naturally usi page pe appear hota hai. Jab Main Window multiple pages mein flow karta hai, Final Window automatically last page se attach hota hai. Mere invoice project mein Final Window grand total, tax summary, aur authorised signatory text dikhata tha. Business value manual page tracking ya post-processing ke bina automatic end-of-document content placement hai.
forms-workflow-030.interviewScriptEnglish: A Final Window in SmartForms renders content only on the last page of the form output. It is typically used for grand totals, signature line, terms and conditions footer, or stamp areas. In a single-page form the Final Window naturally appears on that page. When the Main Window flows across multiple pages, the Final Window automatically attaches to the last page. In my invoice project the Final Window showed the grand total, tax summary, and authorised signatory text. The business value is automatic end-of-document content placement without manual page tracking or post-processing.

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Placing totals at the end of the Main Window.
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Totals render right after the last item, possibly mid-page, not at bottom of last page.
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use Final Window for totals so they always appear cleanly on the last page.

commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use Final Window which has built-in last-page semantics.

forms-workflow-030.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Final Window in SmartForms is a special window that renders only on the last page of the form output.

Used for:

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens to the Final Window in a single-page Smart Form output?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab Smart Form output single page pe fit ho jaata hai, woh page simultaneously first aur last page hai. Final Window us pe render hota hai kyunki yeh last page hai. Yeh correct aur expected behavior hai — single-page invoice ko abhi bhi signature block show karna chahiye. Koi special configuration ya driver code nahi chahiye; Final Window automatically single-page aur multi-page scenarios dono handle karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When Smart Form output fits on a single page, that page is simultaneously the first and last page. The Final Window renders on it since it is the last page. This is correct and expected behavior — a single-page invoice should still show the signature block. No special configuration or driver code is needed; the Final Window handles both single-page and multi-page scenarios automatically.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Invoice with 2 items — single page output. Final Window (signature block) correctly appeared at bottom. Invoice with 50 items — 3 pages. Final Window appeared only on page 3. Same form, same configuration, both scenarios handled correctly automatically.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Assume karna ki Final Window sirf multi-page documents mein relevant hai. Single page documents mein bhi Final Window chahiye hoti hai signature block ke liye — aur woh correctly kaam karti hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Test Final Window for both scenarios:

" Scenario 1: Few items → single page output
" SELECT 2 items from VBAP
" Call form → Output: 1 page with signature block (Final Window)

" Scenario 2: Many items → multi-page output
" SELECT 50 items from VBAP
" Call form → Output:
"   Page 1: Items 1-20, no signature
"   Page 2: Items 21-40, no signature
"   Page 3: Items 41-50 + signature block (Final Window)

" Same driver code, same form — Final Window auto-detects last page
" Driver does NOT need to know how many pages will be generated
" Smart Forms runtime handles page count internally

followupAnswerBank.question: Can the Final Window appear on an extra blank page if main content fills all pages?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, agar Main Window content last page ko exactly fill kar deta hai Final Window ke liye space nahi chhodte hue, Smart Forms ek additional blank page generate kar sakta hai sirf Final Window content contain karte hue. Ise prevent karne ke liye, main carefully Main Window height size karta hoon har page ke bottom pe Final Window ke liye sufficient space chhodne ke liye. Yeh Final Window content ke approximate size jaan na aur Main Window height ko uske liye account karke set karna require karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, if Main Window content exactly fills the last page leaving no room for the Final Window, Smart Forms may generate an additional blank page containing only the Final Window content. To prevent this, I carefully size the Main Window height to leave sufficient space at the bottom of each page for the Final Window. This requires knowing the approximate size of the Final Window content and setting the Main Window height to account for it.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Invoice form mein signature block ek extra blank page pe aa raha tha. Analysis: Main Window height poori page tak extend thi. Fix: Main Window height 24cm se 20cm ki, Final Window ke liye 4cm space ensure kiya. Signature block last page pe content ke saath correctly aa gaya.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Main Window height maximum rakhna (full page height) — Final Window ke liye space nahi bachti, extra page banta hai. Always reserve space for Final Window by limiting Main Window height.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Page layout calculation to prevent extra Final Window page:

" A4 page: 29.7cm height
" Margins: Top=1cm, Bottom=1cm → usable = 27.7cm
" Header (Secondary Window): 3cm
" Footer (Secondary Window): 1cm
" Final Window height: 4cm

" WRONG: Main Window height = 23.7cm (full remaining space)
" Risk: content fills 23.7cm → no space for 4cm Final Window
" → extra blank page generated for Final Window

" CORRECT: Main Window height = 19.7cm (reserve 4cm for Final Window)
" Page layout:
"   Header:       Top=1cm,    Height=3cm
"   Main Window:  Top=4cm,    Height=19.7cm  ← reduced
"   Final Window: Top=23.7cm, Height=4cm
"   Footer:       Top=27.7cm, Height=0.7cm
" Result: Final Window always has space on last page

followupAnswerBank.question: What content is typically placed in a Final Window for an invoice form?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Invoice Final Window ke liye, typical content include karta hai: words mein amount (often invoice total se programmatically generated), account number aur IFSC code ke saath payment bank details, company seal placeholder aur signature line ke saath authorized signatory block, agar tax regulations require karte hain toh statutory declaration, aur kabhi customer receipt acknowledgment section. Yeh elements logically sirf document end pe placed hain — har page pe rakhna incorrect aur confusing hoga.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: For an invoice Final Window, typical content includes: amount in words (often programmatically generated from the invoice total), payment bank details with account number and IFSC code, the authorized signatory block with company seal placeholder and signature line, a statutory declaration if required by tax regulations, and sometimes a customer receipt acknowledgment section. These elements are logically placed at document end only — putting them on every page would be incorrect and confusing.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: GST invoice requirement: FINAL_WIN mein amount in words (ABAP code node to convert amount to words), bank details for NEFT/RTGS payment, signatory block with pre-printed company name, aur GST declaration text. Sab logically last page pe — correct presentation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Amount in words ko header Secondary Window mein daalna — amount calculation form mein tabhi possible hai jab table iteration complete ho, jo last page tak hi pata chalta hai. Amount in words Final Window mein ya end of Main Window mein daalna correct hai.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does Final Window interact with Copy Window in Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Final Window aur Copy Window different dimensions ke saath independently operate karte hain. Final Window page position ke basis pe activate hota hai — sirf last page. Copy Window copy number ke basis pe activate hota hai — saari non-original copies. Duplicate copy ke last page pe, dono windows simultaneously active hain: Final Window se signature block aur Copy Window se COPY stamp dono appear hote hain. Yeh combined rendering require karta hai ki dono windows visually ek dusre ke saath page pe conflict na karne ke liye positioned hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Final Window and Copy Window operate independently along different dimensions. Final Window activates based on page position — last page only. Copy Window activates based on copy number — all non-original copies. On the last page of a duplicate copy, both windows are active simultaneously: the signature block from Final Window and the COPY stamp from Copy Window both appear. This combined rendering requires that the two windows are positioned to not visually conflict with each other on the page.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: 3-copy 2-page invoice test kiya. Original page 2: Final Window (signature) dikh raha tha, Copy Window nahi. Copy 2 page 2: Final Window (signature) + Copy Window ('COPY' stamp) dono saath dikh rahe the. Both windows positioned non-overlapping — clean output.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Ek ke hone se doosri automatically disable nahi hoti — dono independent hain. Design mein ensure karo ki COPY stamp aur signature block spatially overlap na karein.

" +--------------------------------------------------+
" |  FINAL WINDOW: Signature block     | COPY_WIN:   |
" |  'Authorized Signatory ____'       | 'COPY'      |
" |  Bank Details                      | (top-right) |
" +--------------------------------------------------+
" |  FOOTER (Secondary Window: page number)          |

" COPY_WIN:  Left=16cm, Top=24cm, Width=3cm,  Height=4cm

followupAnswerBank.question: Can you put ABAP code nodes inside a Final Window in Smart Forms?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, ABAP code nodes Final Window ke andar supported hain dusre Smart Forms ke kisi bhi window ki tarah. Yeh last-page-specific logic ke liye useful hai jaise accumulated totals se words mein total amount calculate karna, company code ke basis pe appropriate authorized signatory select karna, ya customer payment method ke basis pe dynamic payment terms render karna. ABAP code sirf tab execute hota hai jab Final Window render hota hai — that is, sirf last page pe.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, ABAP code nodes are supported inside Final Window just like any other window in Smart Forms. This is useful for last-page-specific logic such as calculating total amount in words from accumulated totals, selecting the appropriate authorized signatory based on company code, or rendering dynamic payment terms based on customer payment method. The ABAP code executes only when the Final Window is rendered — that is, only on the last page.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Invoice Final Window mein ABAP code node likha jo amount in words calculate karta tha. GV_TOTAL (accumulated across all items in Main Window table iteration) ko words mein convert kiya using custom FM. Result sirf last page pe amount in words text mein print hua.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Final Window ke ABAP code node mein global variables use karna jo Main Window table iteration mein set hue hon — ensure karo ki woh variables correctly populated hain before Final Window renders. Main Window completes first, Final Window renders after.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" ABAP code node inside Final Window:
" Executed only on last page — after all Main Window content done

" Global variable accumulated during Main Window table iteration:
" GV_TOTAL_AMOUNT type DMBTR  ← set in table body loop

" ABAP code node in Final Window:
DATA lv_amount_words TYPE string.

" Convert total amount to words
CALL FUNCTION 'SPELL_AMOUNT'
  EXPORTING
    amount   = gv_total_amount
    currency = gs_header-waerk
    language = sy-langu
  IMPORTING
    in_words = lv_amount_words
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS = 1.

" Store result in global variable for text node
gv_amount_words = lv_amount_words.

" Text node below code node uses:
" 'Total Amount in Words: &GV_AMOUNT_WORDS&'

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between Final Window and placing content at the end of the Main Window?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Main Window ke end pe placed content last table row ke immediately baad render hota hai — jo data volume ke basis pe kisi bhi page pe ho sakta hai. Agar 3 items page 1 pe fit hote hain, signature block 3 items ke right baad page 1 pe appear hoga. Final Window ke saath, signature block hamesha absolute last page pe fixed position pe appear hota hai, chahe Main Window content kahan end ho. Final Window positional guarantee deta hai; content-at-end-of-Main-Window sequential guarantee deta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Content placed at the end of the Main Window renders immediately after the last table row — which could be on any page depending on data volume. If 3 items fit on page 1, the signature block would appear on page 1 right after the 3 items. With a Final Window, the signature block always appears at a fixed position on the absolute last page, regardless of where the Main Window content ends. Final Window gives positional guarantee; content-at-end-of-Main-Window gives sequential guarantee.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Pehle signature block Main Window ke andar last mein raka tha. 2-item invoice pe page 1 ke middle mein signature block aa gaya — page bottom pe nahi. Visually wrong lagta tha. Final Window use kiya — signature block hamesha page ke neeche, last page pe. Correct presentation.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Main Window ke andar signature place karna assuming it will be at the bottom of the last page — yeh sirf tab sahi hoga jab content exactly page fill kare. Variable content ke saath Final Window use karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Comparison of approaches:

" APPROACH 1: Signature at end of Main Window
" Main Window content:
"   [Table header]
"   [Item row 1]      ← if only 2 items, form ends here on page 1
"   [Item row 2]
"   [Signature block] ← appears immediately after last item
"                        may be mid-page if few items!

" APPROACH 2: Signature in Final Window
" Main Window content:
"   [Table header]
"   [Item row 1]      ← 2 items: ends mid-page 1
"   [Item row 2]
"                     ← rest of Main Window space is empty
" Final Window (bottom of last page, fixed position):
"   [Signature block] ← always at defined Y position on last page

" Use Final Window for:
" - Documents where signature must be at page bottom
" - Content that must appear after ALL data is printed
" - Consistent last-page layout regardless of data volume

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test that the Final Window appears only on the last page?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Final Window behavior test karne ke liye, main form ko do different data volumes ke saath run karta hoon. Pehle minimal data ke saath single-page output produce karta hoon — Final Window us single page pe appear hona chahiye. Phir large data ke saath multi-page output produce karta hoon — Final Window sirf last page pe appear hona chahiye aur saare preceding pages se completely absent hona chahiye. Main yeh print preview ke through page by page scroll karke verify karta hoon. Agar window har page pe appear hota hai, woh Final Window ke bajaye Secondary Window ke roop mein create hua tha — check karne ke liye common mistake.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: To test Final Window behavior, I run the form with two different data volumes. First with minimal data producing single-page output — the Final Window should appear on that single page. Then with large data producing multi-page output — the Final Window should appear only on the last page and be completely absent from all preceding pages. I verify this by scrolling through print preview page by page. If the window appears on every page, it was created as Secondary Window instead of Final Window — a common mistake to check for.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: After form development, test driver mein SELECT top 5 items karke single-page test, phir SELECT * for all items multi-page test. Print preview mein page navigation use kiya. Both scenarios correct — Final Window only on last page confirmed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Sirf single-page test karna — Final Window aur Secondary Window dono single-page pe dikhenge. Multi-page test mandatory hai distinguish karne ke liye. Always test with enough data to generate at least 3 pages.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Test procedure for Final Window validation:
REPORT ztest_final_window.

DATA: lv_fm   TYPE rs38l_fnam,
      ls_ctrl TYPE ssfctrlop,
      lt_items TYPE TABLE OF vbap.

CALL FUNCTION 'SSF_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME'
  EXPORTING  formname = 'ZINVOICE_FORM'
  IMPORTING  fm_name  = lv_fm
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS   = 1.

ls_ctrl-preview = abap_true.

" TEST CASE 1: Single page output
SELECT * FROM vbap INTO TABLE @lt_items
  WHERE vbeln = '0000012345' UP TO 3 ROWS.
" Call form → Verify: Final Window appears on page 1 (only page)

" TEST CASE 2: Multi-page output
SELECT * FROM vbap INTO TABLE @lt_items
  WHERE vbeln = '0000012345'.  " All items
" Call form → Scroll to intermediate pages: Final Window absent
"           → Scroll to last page: Final Window present
" If Final Window on intermediate pages → it is SecondaryWindow, fix type!

followupAnswerBank.question: Can a Final Window also serve as a watermark on the last page?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, Final Window last-page-only stamp ya watermark ke roop mein use ho sakta hai. For example, government ya legal documents mein, CERTIFIED ya APPROVED stamp jo sirf last page pe appear hona chahiye Final Window mein placed ho sakta hai. Similarly, document authenticity verification ke liye QR code jo document end pe sirf ek baar appear hona chahiye Final Window mein perfectly fit hota hai. Yeh last page detect karne ke liye conditional ABAP logic likhne ke comparison mein ek clean declarative approach hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, Final Window can be used as a last-page-only stamp or watermark. For example, in government or legal documents, a CERTIFIED or APPROVED stamp that should only appear on the last page can be placed in the Final Window. Similarly, a QR code for document authenticity verification that should only appear once at document end fits perfectly in a Final Window. This is a clean declarative approach compared to writing conditional ABAP logic to detect the last page.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Customs clearance document requirement: 'CUSTOMS VERIFIED' stamp sirf last page pe, diagonally placed. Final Window use kiya with large rotated text. Clean implementation — no page counting logic in ABAP.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Watermark-style content ko Secondary Window mein rakhna agar sirf last page pe chahiye — Secondary Window har page pe dikhegi. Final Window specifically last-page-only content ke liye use karo.

forms-workflow-031.easyUnderstanding: Simple words mein samjho: Smart Forms mein do main window types hain — Main Window aur Secondary Window — aur dono ka role bilkul alag hai. Socho ek newspaper: header (newspaper name, date, edition) — woh har page pe same position pe hota hai, fixed, never moves. Yeh Secondary Window hai. Articles aur news content — woh flow karte hain, ek page se doosre page pe, jitna bhi content ho. Yeh Main Window hai. Main Window ki khas baat: woh content ke saath grow karta hai. 5 invoice items — Main Window 5 rows lete hai. 500 items — Main Window automatically 10 pages bhar deta hai. Secondary Window ki khas baat: woh fixed jagah pe fixed size mein baitta rehta hai. Company logo always top-right, 4cm x 3cm — kabhi badlega nahi, kabhi hilega nahi. Ek page pe dono ek saath hote hain — lekin dono ke purposes completely different hain. Interview mein jab yeh question poochha jaaye — Main Window = dynamic, grows, flows across pages; Secondary Window = static, fixed, same page same position.
forms-workflow-031.hinglishMasterExplanation:
Interview mein Main vs Secondary Window comparison poochha jaaye toh structured comparison do:

1. Size and Position:
   - Main Window: Position aur width define hoti hai, lekin HEIGHT dynamic hai — content ke saath grow karti hai. Next page pe bhi continue ho sakti hai.
   - Secondary Window: Size aur position completely fixed — X, Y, Width, Height sab design time pe define, runtime pe change nahi hota.

2. Content Overflow:
   - Main Window: Content overflow → automatically next page pe continue hota hai. Table mein 1000 rows bhi ho — sab print honge across multiple pages.
   - Secondary Window: Content overflow → extra content silently cut off. Koi error nahi. Isliye sirf bounded content rakho.

3. Page Occurrence:
   - Main Window: Ek form mein exactly ONE Main Window hota hai. Yeh mandatory hai multi-page forms ke liye.
   - Secondary Window: Multiple Secondary Windows ho sakti hain same page pe — logo window, header window, address window, footer window, sab alag-alag.

4. Content Type:
   - Main Window: Table/repeating data — invoice line items, PO line items, delivery items.
   - Secondary Window: Static fixed content — company name, logo, page number, document title.

5. Page Range:
   - Main Window: Har assigned page pe hai (continues across pages).
   - Secondary Window: Sirf specific pages pe — configurable per page node.

6. Design Time vs Runtime:
   - Main Window height → determined by content at runtime.
   - Secondary Window height → fixed at design time, never changes.

7. Practical Rule of Thumb:
   'Kya content ki length runtime pe vary kar sakti hai?' — YES → Main Window. NO → Secondary Window.
forms-workflow-031.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'The Main Window in Smart Forms is dynamic — it grows with content and automatically continues to the next page when content overflows. It is used for repeating data like invoice line items or purchase order positions. There can be exactly one Main Window in a form, and it is mandatory for multi-page output. The Secondary Window has a fixed size and position defined at design time and does not overflow — if content exceeds the boundary, it is clipped. Secondary Windows are used for static content like company logo, document header, and page number footer. Multiple Secondary Windows can coexist on a single page. Both window types appear on the same page simultaneously but serve completely different purposes.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein ek clean one-liner yaad rakho: 'Main Window grows with data and flows across pages — Secondary Window is fixed size and fixed position, never changes.' Phir use case example do: 'Invoice line items ke liye Main Window, company logo ke liye Secondary Window.' Yeh concise aur precise answer hai.
forms-workflow-031.codeExamples:
" === Main Window vs Secondary Window — Design Notes and Driver ===

" SMARTFORMS Page Layout Configuration:

" SECONDARY WINDOWS (fixed, static content):
" LOGO_WIN:
"   Type: Secondary Window
"   Position: Left=15cm, Top=0.5cm
"   Width: 4cm, Height: 2.5cm
"   Content: Graphic node (company logo from BDS/SE78)

" HEADER_WIN:
"   Type: Secondary Window
"   Position: Left=1cm, Top=0.5cm
"   Width: 13cm, Height: 2.5cm
"   Content: Company name, address, GSTIN text nodes

" FOOTER_WIN:
"   Type: Secondary Window
"   Position: Left=1cm, Top=27.5cm
"   Width: 19cm, Height: 0.7cm
"   Content: 'Page &SFSY-PAGE& of &SFSY-FORMPAGES&'

" MAIN WINDOW (dynamic, growing content):
" MAIN:
"   Type: Main Window
"   Position: Left=1cm, Top=4cm
"   Width: 19cm
"   Height: 23cm  ← this is MAX per page; actual grows with content
"   Content:
"     Table node → Header area (column titles — prints once)
"     Table node → Body area (loops over IT_ITEMS)
"       Line node → &IT_ITEMS-POSNR& &IT_ITEMS-MATNR& &IT_ITEMS-KWMENG&
"     Table node → Footer area (totals — prints at table end)

" Driver Program:
DATA: lv_fm_name TYPE rs38l_fnam,
      ls_ctrl    TYPE ssfctrlop,
      ls_vbak    TYPE vbak,
      lt_vbap    TYPE TABLE OF vbap.

CALL FUNCTION 'SSF_FUNCTION_MODULE_NAME'
  EXPORTING  formname = 'ZINVOICE_FORM'
  IMPORTING  fm_name  = lv_fm_name
  EXCEPTIONS OTHERS   = 1.

ls_ctrl-preview   = abap_true.
ls_ctrl-no_dialog = abap_false.

" Both Secondary Windows and Main Window handled by form design
" Driver just passes data — form decides which window to use
CALL FUNCTION lv_fm_name
  EXPORTING
    control_parameters = ls_ctrl
    is_header          = ls_vbak   " Used in HEADER_WIN
  TABLES
    it_items           = lt_vbap   " Used in MAIN Window table loop
  EXCEPTIONS
    OTHERS             = 1.
forms-workflow-031.interviewScriptHinglish: Main vs Secondary Window foundational SmartForm concept hai. Main Window per form unique hota hai, pages mein overflow support karta hai, aur primary body content host karta hai. Secondary Windows kayi ho sakte hain, per page fixed-position hote hain, aur overflow nahi karte. Tables aur narratives jaise data-driven content ke liye Main Window use karo, aur logos, headers, aur footers jaise static content ke liye Secondary Windows. Page breaks Main Window overflow se trigger hote hain; Secondary Windows automatically har generated page pe replicate hote hain. Mere delivery note mein Main Window ne items table hold ki jabki Secondary Windows ne company branding hold ki. Yeh clear split multi-page rendering ko predictable rakhta hai.
forms-workflow-031.interviewScriptEnglish: Main vs Secondary Window is a foundational SmartForm concept. The Main Window is unique per form, supports overflow across pages, and hosts the primary body content. Secondary Windows can be many, are fixed-position per page, and do not overflow. Use the Main Window for data-driven content like tables and narratives, and Secondary Windows for static content such as logos, headers, and footers. Page breaks are triggered by Main Window overflow; Secondary Windows are automatically replicated on each generated page. In my delivery note the Main Window held the items table while Secondary Windows held company branding. This clear split keeps multi-page rendering predictable.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Unique, overflow-capable window for primary body content.

forms-workflow-031.quickRevisionNotes:
What: SmartForms distinguishes Main Window (unique, overflow) from Secondary Window (many, fixed) for predictable multi-page rendering.

Used for:
- Choosing where to place content (data vs static)
- Multi-page invoice and delivery layouts
- Headers, footers, logos placement
